## source_type: ## band_count
## age_range_hint
england_rshe_secondary: ages 11-16 (DfE RSHE statutory guidance; secondary phase = Years 7-11, KS3 + KS4; outcomes framed as 'by the end of secondary school')

## source_type: ## detection_confidence
## progression_philosophy
england_rshe_secondary: The DfE RSHE statutory guidance sets outcomes as 'by the end of secondary school' terminal statements. Unlike the National Curriculum, which has Key Stage progression, RSHE uses a single secondary-phase endpoint. Single-band output reflects the guidance's own structure. Per DfE statutory RSHE guidance (England), Children and Social Work Act 2017.

## source_type: ## band_detail | End of Secondary
column4: Secondary pupils develop the knowledge, values and personal qualities needed to navigate relationships, health and wellbeing across the full secondary phase. Outcomes are framed as terminal goals for all secondary pupils; year-group sequencing is a school decision.
